scope: Security, Supply Chain, and Observability audit of Jekyll site navigation/rendering; focus files: _data/navigation.yml, _pages/trading.html, _pages/live-trading.html, _config.yml, _includes/head/custom.html, Gemfile, assets/css/main.scss

findings.title: CDN Scripts Loaded Without Subresource Integrity (SRI)
findings.claim: External JavaScript loaded from a CDN should be verified against a known hash to prevent supply chain attacks
findings.reality: Both pages load Chart.js from jsDelivr CDN without integrity or crossorigin attributes
findings.impact: If jsDelivr is compromised, arbitrary JavaScript executes in visitor browsers on the yfeng0206.github.io origin

findings.title: innerHTML Used With Future-External Data Source, No Sanitization
findings.claim: Data rendered into DOM should be sanitized, especially when migration to external data source is planned
findings.reality: render() builds HTML strings by concatenating data properties into 5 innerHTML calls with zero sanitization; migration to external fetch is documented in comments
findings.impact: Currently safe (inline data). After planned migration to external JSON source, stored XSS via compromised data file

findings.title: Google Fonts Loaded Without SRI
findings.reality: Google Fonts CSS loaded without integrity attribute; SRI is impractical for dynamically-generated Google Fonts CSS
findings.impact: Theoretical: compromised Google Fonts could inject malicious CSS. Practically very unlikely.

findings.title: Gemfile.lock Excluded From Version Control
findings.claim: Reproducible builds require pinned dependency versions via Gemfile.lock
findings.reality: Gemfile.lock is gitignored, Gemfile has zero version pins. github-pages meta-gem pulls ~85+ transitive dependencies resolved fresh each build.
findings.impact: Non-reproducible builds; supply chain risk from unpinned transitive dependencies

findings.title: No Error Handling for CDN Script Load Failure
findings.claim: Failure to load a critical external script should be detectable by the user
findings.reality: No onerror handler on script tags, no try/catch around Chart instantiation, no fallback UI. If CDN is blocked, pages show empty canvases with no explanation.
findings.impact: Complete visual failure of both trading pages when Chart.js CDN is blocked or down, with no user-facing error

findings.title: Duplicate Chart.js CDN Load Across Pages (No Caching Coordination)
findings.claim: External scripts used across multiple pages should be managed centrally for version consistency
findings.reality: Chart.js version hardcoded independently in two separate files with no mechanism to enforce consistency
findings.impact: Version drift risk if one file is updated and the other is not

findings.title: No Content Security Policy Headers
findings.claim: Sites loading external scripts should declare a Content Security Policy
findings.reality: No CSP defined anywhere -- no _headers file, no meta tag, no netlify.toml. GitHub Pages does not inject CSP by default.
findings.impact: No defense-in-depth against XSS. Any future XSS vulnerability has no browser-level mitigation.

findings.title: render() Has No Error Boundary; Silent Total Failure
findings.claim: An async function building all page content should handle errors gracefully
findings.reality: render() performs 6+ DOM operations in series with no try/catch. Called at line 525 with no .catch(). Any error silently kills subsequent rendering.
findings.impact: Partially or fully empty page with no user-visible error when any rendering step fails

findings.title: Resume PDF and Photo Present in Working Directory
findings.claim: Sensitive files should not be present in deployable directories
findings.reality: Resume 2025.pdf and 1696906260300.jpg are gitignored but present locally; accessible during local jekyll serve
findings.impact: Personal information exposure during local development if server is network-accessible
